howItWorks.intro: This debt-to-income calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Front-End DTI = Housing Payment / Gross Monthly Income × 100
Back-End DTI = Total Monthly Debt / Gross Monthly Income × 100
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is a good DTI ratio?
faq.answer: Most lenders prefer a back-end DTI of 36% or less. FHA loans allow up to 43%, and some lenders go to 50% with strong compensating factors like high credit scores or large reserves.

faq.question: What is front-end vs back-end DTI?
faq.answer: Front-end DTI includes only housing costs. Back-end DTI includes all monthly debt payments. Lenders primarily look at back-end DTI.

faq.question: How do I lower my DTI?
faq.answer: Pay off debts, increase income, or reduce the mortgage amount you are seeking. Even paying off a small car loan can significantly improve your ratio.

faq.question: Does DTI affect my interest rate?
faq.answer: Not directly, but a high DTI may limit you to certain loan programs with higher rates. Lower DTI gives you access to the best rates and terms.

chart.title: DTI Qualification Ranges
